name: Phase 1: Preamble
summary:
The reference verifier's preamble absorbs VK-level metadata (pre-hash, common main commit, per-AIR presence, preprocessed/cached commitments, public values) into the Fiat-Shamir transcript, validates proof-shape bounds, and derives the sort order plus aggregate quantities (n_logup, n_max, num_present_airs, total-interactions bound) that downstream phases consume.

In the circuit this work is discharged by two AIRs. ProofShapeAir has one row per child AIR indexed by sorted_idx, plus a summary (is_last) row that aggregates per-proof quantities and emits the module-handoff messages. PublicValuesAir has one row per public value, receives the count from ProofShapeAir through NumPublicValuesBus, and forwards each observation to the constraint evaluator. Together they replay every transcript observation in the reference verifier's preamble block and broadcast the derived metadata on AirShapeBus, AirPresenceBus, CommitmentsBus, HyperdimBus, LiftedHeightsBus, and the per-proof module buses.

blocks.html: Two AIRs carry this phase. We start with ProofShapeAir, which is the heavier of the two: it enforces the row-per-AIR structure, replays every transcript observation from the reference verifier's preamble, runs the trace-height bound on its summary row, and emits the handoff messages that downstream modules consume. PublicValuesAir follows, handling the per-public-value transcript observations and the forward to the constraint evaluator.

blocks.heading: ProofShapeAir
blocks.html: ProofShapeAir walks once through the child proof's AIRs in sort order. Each row selects one entry from the VK metadata table via a one-hot idx_flags encoder, conditionally observes transcript values at precomputed tidx positions, and emits the per-AIR data messages. The final is_last row is a summary row that closes out per-proof aggregates (n_logup, n_max, the total-interactions bound) and emits the module-handoff messages.

blocks.heading: Cols struct (fixed part)
blocks.explain: These are the fixed columns. proof_idx is the per-proof bus key, is_valid marks AIR rows (versus padding), and is_first/is_last bound the per-proof window. Note the dual use pattern: on regular rows log_height is the AIR's log trace height and height is 2^log_height, but on the summary row (is_last) the same cells are reused as scratch for the n_logup check (see the summary-row block below, which aliases height as limb_to_range_check and log_height as msb_limb_zero_bits_exp). starting_tidx and starting_cidx are per-row running indices into the transcript and commitments list; they're threaded forward via internal permutation buses so transcript observations land at the correct absolute positions.

blocks.heading: Variable-length columns (idx_flags, cached_commits)
blocks.explain: After the fixed columns the trace holds two variable-length blocks whose widths depend on the child VK. idx_flags is the encoder that picks which AIR this row represents (one-hot plus encoder slack); cached_commits is a DIGEST_SIZE-wide entry per cached commitment slot (up to max_cached). These are the columns that hold the actual hash digests observed on TranscriptBus and registered on CommitmentsBus.

blocks.heading: Borrow the row and run the idx encoder
blocks.explain: Setup. The AIR reads a two-row window (local, next) because many constraints are transitions. The idx encoder (a shared primitive) validates that idx_flags is a well-formed one-hot selection; when the row is valid we additionally require the selection is in range. This is what lets later code write self.idx_encoder.get_flag_expr::<AB>(i, localv.idx_flags) and trust it as the indicator that this row represents child AIR i.

blocks.heading: Per-proof nested loop and proof_idx continuity
blocks.explain: The nested-for-loop subair packages the window of (AIR rows + one summary row) per proof into a loop counter proof_idx, with is_first marking the first AIR row of the proof. Inside a proof, proof_idx does not change. is_present is asserted boolean (discharges the PS1a obligation for presence flags in preamble-3) and implies is_valid. n_logup is held constant across all rows of a proof so every per-row send carries the same value the summary row computes.

blocks.heading: num_present counter
blocks.explain: num_present accumulates the count of present AIRs as the loop advances. The first row seeds it with is_present, and each transition adds next.is_present when next is still an AIR row. By the summary row this equals the total number of present AIRs, which is later sent on FractionFolderInputBus (block preamble-10).

blocks.heading: VK field selection setup
blocks.explain: The AIR does not embed the child VK as an array lookup; instead, for every row it folds the VK metadata into a set of per-row expressions by summing over i with the one-hot selector is_current_air. These zeroed accumulators will be populated in the loop below and then used on the same row as either transcript values (preprocessed_commit, num_pvs) or as parameters to bus sends (air_idx, num_interactions, need_rot, widths).

blocks.heading: VK selection loop: width / pvs / interactions
blocks.explain: For each candidate AIR index i, the expression is_current_air is 1 iff this row represents AIR i. Multiplying VK constants by this indicator and summing across i effectively indexes into the VK: after the loop, air_idx holds i, num_interactions holds the child's interaction count for that AIR, num_pvs holds its public-value count, main_common_width holds its main width, and num_interactions_per_row holds the per-limb decomposition that the carry check below uses to multiply by lifted_height.

blocks.heading: VK selection loop: required / preprocessed / cached
blocks.explain: The rest of the VK selection loop. is_required discharges the PS1b obligation for preamble-3: if the child VK marks an AIR as required, the one-hot branch asserts is_present = 1 on that row, so required AIRs cannot be omitted. For AIRs with preprocessed data we pin log_height to the VK-fixed value (preprocessed traces have a fixed hypercube dim) and fold the preprocessed commit digest into preprocessed_commit. cached_present[cached_idx] ends up as 1 for AIRs that actually have that cached slot, which later gates the per-slot transcript observations and commitments-bus sends.

blocks.heading: Sort permutation and absent-AIR zeroing
blocks.explain: Two-way send/receive on ProofShapePermutationBus (scoped per proof_idx) establishes that the mapping sorted_idx -> air_idx is a bijection on [0, num_airs). The first AIR row has sorted_idx = 0, and as long as the next row's sorted_idx is non-zero the constraint sorted_idx + 1 = next.sorted_idx increments by exactly one. Absent AIRs are forced to have height = 0 and log_height = 0, which (combined with the non-increasing ordering below) pushes them to the end of the row range.

blocks.heading: Non-increasing log_height
blocks.explain: The sort's primary key. Range-checking local.log_height - next.log_height into [0, 32) forces local.log_height >= next.log_height, i.e. non-increasing heights across consecutive AIR rows. Combined with the zero-height constraint on absent AIRs above, absent rows must sort after every present row. This is exactly the preamble-9 sort condition the reference verifier computes as Sort by (is_none, Reverse(log_height), air_id).

blocks.heading: First-row starting_tidx and per-row handoff
blocks.explain: The row for air_idx == 0 (the first AIR in the per_air order, not in sort order) must start at tidx = 2 * DIGEST_SIZE — that's immediately after the pre-hash (DIGEST_SIZE) and common_main_commit (DIGEST_SIZE) which live at the start of the transcript. The starting_tidx_bus receive/send pair threads the running tidx cursor from row to row keyed by air_idx, so transcript observations emitted later in this row land at absolute positions that chain correctly.

blocks.heading: Observe is_present (when not required)
blocks.explain: First transcript observation per AIR: when the AIR is not VK-required, the prover-supplied is_present flag is absorbed on TranscriptBus with is_sample = 0. Required AIRs skip this observation (their is_present is fixed to 1 by the earlier constraint). The running tidx cursor is conditionally incremented by the same predicate so the next observation lines up correctly.

blocks.heading: Observe preprocessed_commit or log_height
blocks.explain: Mutually exclusive observations per AIR: AIRs that have preprocessed data contribute DIGEST_SIZE transcript slots for the VK-fixed preprocessed_commit; AIRs that don't contribute one slot for the prover-supplied log_height. Both are gated on is_present. This is the split that preamble-4 describes: either the digest or the height scalar goes in, never both. The tidx cursor advances by exactly the right count in each branch.

blocks.heading: Observe cached_commits and reserve PV slots
blocks.explain: For each cached-commit slot that this AIR actually uses (cached_present[i] == 1), all DIGEST_SIZE digest elements are absorbed on TranscriptBus. The cached digests live in the variable columns localv.cached_commits[i]. After that we record num_pvs_tidx (the first transcript index for this AIR's public values) before reserving num_pvs slots for PublicValuesAir to fill in, and finally send (air_idx + 1, tidx) on starting_tidx_bus so the next row's handoff matches.

blocks.heading: Pre-hash observation on the summary row
blocks.explain: These are the two preamble observations that live outside the per-AIR loop. The summary row (is_last) observes the VK pre-hash at tidx = 0..DIGEST_SIZE, sourced from the last cached-commit slot of the summary row (trace generation writes the pre-hash there). The row with is_min_cached (the smallest-indexed AIR that owns a cached commit) observes common_main_commit at tidx = DIGEST_SIZE..2*DIGEST_SIZE, sharing the same cell. This is what makes the transcript start with [vk_pre_hash, common_main_commit].

blocks.heading: AirShapeBus: air_id, num_interactions, need_rot
blocks.explain: Three entries are published on AirShapeBus per present row, keyed by sorted_idx plus a property_idx tag: the underlying air_id, the child AIR's num_interactions, and the need_rot flag. AirPresenceBus separately carries (air_idx, is_present) for every row (present or absent) so downstream modules can look up presence by raw air_idx. The multiplicities are chosen so the number of downstream lookups balances exactly — e.g. the NeedRot row is looked up once per column of width, and num_air_id_lookups is a prover-supplied count used by the symbolic-expression AIR.

blocks.heading: HyperdimBus: signed n
blocks.explain: Each AIR's hypercube dimension is n = log_height - l_skip, which can be negative for very small traces. We decompose it into (n_abs, n_sign_bit) where n_sign_bit is boolean, range-check n_abs into [0, 32), and broadcast (n_abs, n_sign_bit) on HyperdimBus keyed by sorted_idx. This is the signed hypercube dimension that GKR / stacking need per trace.

blocks.heading: Lifted height: main stacking column
blocks.explain: lifted_height = max(height, 2^l_skip) is the ceiling-to-skip-bound version of the trace height used by the stacking module. The PowerCheckerBus lookup does double duty: it range-checks log_height into [0, 32) and simultaneously pins height = 2^log_height via the PowerCheckerAir primitive table. The first LiftedHeightsBus entry (part_idx = 0, commit_idx = 0) covers the main common-trace stacked commitment, multiplied by main_common_width so stacking sees one lookup per column.

blocks.heading: Lifted height: preprocessed commit entry
blocks.explain: For AIRs with preprocessed data, a separate LiftedHeightsBus entry covers the preprocessed-trace part (gated on preprocessed_stacked_width), and CommitmentsBus registers preprocessed_commit at (major_idx=0, minor_idx=starting_cidx + cidx_offset). The commit_mult multiplicity lines up with how many times MerkleVerifyAir looks this root up during WHIR. starting_cidx is the running commitment index threaded across rows; the first AIR row starts at 1 (slot 0 being reserved for the common-main commit).

blocks.heading: Per-cached-slot lifted heights and commitments
blocks.explain: For each cached-commit slot the AIR registers both a LiftedHeightsBus entry (stacked as its own part) and a CommitmentsBus entry with the digest from localv.cached_commits[cached_idx]. The crucial invariant is that the same cached_commits cell feeds both the earlier TranscriptBus observation (preamble block) and this CommitmentsBus send — so the digest the Fiat-Shamir sponge absorbs is bound to be the digest that MerkleVerifyAir later authenticates against. When continuations are enabled, CachedCommitBus additionally forwards the digest out to the enclosing circuit.

blocks.heading: Chain starting_cidx and common-main stacking root
blocks.explain: The transition constraint threads starting_cidx forward: the next row's starting commit index equals this row's starting index plus the commits this row consumed (cidx_offset). Separately, the row carrying is_min_cached publishes common_main_commit on CommitmentsBus at minor_idx = 0, reusing the last cached-commit slot as its storage cell — the same cell that was observed on TranscriptBus in the summary-row block above.

blocks.heading: PreHashBus publication (continuations)
blocks.explain: When continuations are on, the summary row also sends the VK pre-hash out on PreHashBus. The source cell is again the last cached-commit slot (cached_commits[max_cached - 1]), the same cell that supplies the pre-hash observation at tidx = 0..DIGEST_SIZE. Because a single cell drives both outputs, whatever the enclosing circuit verifies about the pre-hash public value must also be what was absorbed into the transcript.

blocks.heading: NumPublicValuesBus handoff
blocks.explain: For each present AIR with a non-zero public-value count, ProofShapeAir sends (air_idx, tidx = num_pvs_tidx, num_pvs) on NumPublicValuesBus. num_pvs_tidx was captured earlier (right before the public-value slots were reserved in the transcript), and num_pvs is the VK-fixed count folded in by the selection loop. PublicValuesAir will receive this message and unroll it into one row per public value, each at the correct tidx.

blocks.html: The remaining blocks handle the per-proof summary row (is_last): validating the lifted-height limb decomposition, the incremental total-interactions accumulator, the n_logup leading-zero check, the n_max aggregation, and the final module handoffs on GkrModuleBus, ExpressionClaimNMaxBus, NLiftBus, Eq3bShapeBus, EqNsNLogupMaxBus, FractionFolderInputBus, and the strict total-interactions bound.

blocks.heading: Lifted-height limb decomposition
blocks.explain: The scalar lifted_height is decomposed into NUM_LIMBS limbs of LIMB_BITS each, with the weighted sum constrained to equal lifted_height and each limb range-checked into [0, 2^LIMB_BITS). This is the foundation for both the limb-wise multiplication (num_interactions = lifted_height * num_interactions_per_row) in the next block and the final total-interactions bound.

blocks.heading: num_interactions = lifted_height * num_interactions_per_row
blocks.explain: Schoolbook multiplication in limbs: num_interactions_limbs = lifted_height_limbs * num_interactions_per_row. The algorithm forms partial products into a 2*NUM_LIMBS carry accumulator, then for each limb subtracts the claimed output limb, divides by the base, and range-checks the resulting carry. The final carry is asserted zero, which forces the product to fit in NUM_LIMBS limbs (no overflow). This is how the AIR verifies the child's per-AIR num_interactions value equals lifted_height * (VK-fixed interactions per row).

blocks.heading: Running total_interactions accumulator
blocks.explain: Induction on the running sum: the first row sets total_interactions_limbs = 0, and each transition enforces next.total_interactions_limbs = local.total_interactions_limbs + local.num_interactions_limbs limb-by-limb with a boolean carry. After walking all AIR rows, the summary row's local.total_interactions_limbs equals the full sum sum_i(num_interactions[i] * lifted_height[i]).

blocks.heading: Summary-row boundary constraints
blocks.explain: These constraints pin is_last to the correct row. It must be boolean; it cannot coexist with is_valid; the row before it must be valid; and next.is_last can only be set when local.sorted_idx = per_air.len() - 1, i.e. after the last AIR row. After the summary row we either start the next proof (next.is_first) or land in padding (!next.is_valid && !next.is_last).

blocks.heading: n_logup: column aliasing on summary row
blocks.explain: On the summary row, several regular columns are reinterpreted as scratch for the n_logup leading-zero check — lifted_height_limbs becomes a one-hot non_zero_marker over limb indices, height is the most-significant non-zero limb, n_sign_bit is its inverse witness, and log_height is 2^(leading zeros within that limb). The loop above enforces that the marker selects the highest non-zero limb (prefix = 1 at and after that limb) and that limb_times_inv = 1 whenever a non-zero limb exists.

blocks.heading: n_logup: close out the leading-zero count
blocks.explain: When the total is non-zero, we need the MSB limb, once shifted up by 2^(leading zeros), to sit in [128, 256) — exactly the range that forces n_logup to be the unique exponent such that total_interactions < 2^(n_logup + l_skip). The PowerCheckerBus lookup ties msb_limb_zero_bits (derived from the marker) to msb_limb_zero_bits_exp (= 2^msb_limb_zero_bits), and the pair of range checks on shifted_msb_limb and shifted_msb_limb - 128 confirms the top bit is set. When the total is zero, limb_times_inv = 0 and n_logup is forced to zero.

blocks.heading: n_max accumulation
blocks.explain: n_max is held constant across all rows of the proof (transition constraint), and on the first AIR row it equals max(0, n) where n = log_height - l_skip. Because the sort is non-increasing on log_height, the first AIR row has the largest log_height and therefore the largest n, so its max(0, n) is the maximum across present AIRs — n_max = max_i max(0, n_i). is_n_max_greater is boolean, and on the summary row the signed product (n_max - n_logup) * (2*is_n_max_greater - 1) is range-checked non-negative, i.e. is_n_max_greater = 1 iff n_max > n_logup.

blocks.heading: GkrModuleBus handoff
blocks.explain: The kickoff message for phase 2a (GKR). The summary row's starting_tidx equals tidx_end — the running transcript cursor after every preamble observation. Combined with (n_logup, n_max, is_n_max_greater), this is everything GkrInputAir needs to start sampling alpha_logup and running the fractional-sumcheck reduction. EqNsNLogupMaxBus simultaneously broadcasts (n_logup, n_max) to subsidiary equality-check AIRs that use this pair.

blocks.heading: ExpressionClaimNMaxBus and n_lift sends
blocks.explain: n_lift = max(0, log_height - l_skip), the non-negative component of n. Per present AIR it is sent on NLiftBus keyed by air_idx (BatchConstraint's constraint-folding AIR looks it up) and on Eq3bShapeBus keyed by sorted_idx alongside n_logup and num_interactions. Per-proof, n_max is separately sent on ExpressionClaimNMaxBus for the symbolic-expression AIR to bound its loop.

blocks.heading: FractionFolderInputBus: num_present_airs
blocks.explain: The fraction-folder AIR (part of BatchConstraint) needs to know how many AIRs actually contributed logup fractions to fold. On the summary row num_present equals the total count (since every AIR row added next.is_present), and this message hands it off.

blocks.heading: Strict total-interactions bound
blocks.explain: The strict inequality total_interactions < max_interaction_count. Summary-row num_interactions_limbs is reused as diff_marker, a one-hot selector pointing to the most-significant limb at which the two limb decompositions differ. The inner assertion that prefix = 1 whenever there is a differing limb enforces that the marker picks the highest differing position. diff_val then holds max_interactions[msb] - total_interactions_limbs[msb], and range-checking diff_val - 1 into [0, 2^LIMB_BITS) proves diff_val >= 1, i.e. strict positivity at the MSB, which is exactly total < max. At circuit construction time, max_interaction_count is verified to imply every TraceHeightConstraint in the child VK, so this single bound discharges all of them.

blocks.html: That closes out ProofShapeAir. Its sibling PublicValuesAir is much smaller: one row per public value, receiving the per-AIR count from NumPublicValuesBus and forwarding each observation to PublicValuesBus for the constraint evaluator. This is what makes the transcript slots reserved above (at num_pvs_tidx) line up with actual values.

blocks.heading: PublicValuesAir
blocks.html: PublicValuesAir unrolls each present AIR's public values. It has one row per (air_idx, pv_idx) pair, receives the total count per AIR from ProofShapeAir via NumPublicValuesBus, observes each value on TranscriptBus, and forwards (air_idx, pv_idx, value) on PublicValuesBus for SymbolicExpressionAir to use during constraint evaluation.

blocks.heading: Cols struct
blocks.code:
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow, Debug)]
pub struct PublicValuesCols<F> {
    pub is_valid: F,

    pub proof_idx: F,
    pub air_idx: F,
    pub pv_idx: F,

    pub is_first_in_proof: F,
    pub is_first_in_air: F,

    pub tidx: F,
    pub value: F,
}
blocks.explain: Each row identifies one public value by (proof_idx, air_idx, pv_idx) and carries the transcript index tidx and the value. is_first_in_proof marks the first row per proof (for the nested loop), and is_first_in_air marks the first row per AIR within a proof (so the NumPublicValuesBus receive lands on exactly one row per AIR).

blocks.heading: Nested loop and row-transition checks
blocks.explain: The nested-loop subair windows rows per proof. is_first_in_air is boolean; it's forced to 1 on the first row of each proof, and whenever the next row's air_idx changes (next.air_idx - local.air_idx != 0) the transition forces next.is_first_in_air = 1. Within an AIR run the first row has pv_idx = 0.

blocks.heading: NumPublicValuesBus receive
blocks.explain: The trick here is that the message is received exactly once per AIR run, on its last row (the one where the next row does not continue the same AIR). local.is_valid - is_same_air is 1 only on that row. At that point pv_idx + 1 equals the total num_pvs for this AIR, and tidx - pv_idx recovers the starting transcript index. This matches exactly what ProofShapeAir sent on NumPublicValuesBus: (air_idx, num_pvs_tidx, num_pvs).

blocks.heading: Same-AIR transition: increment pv_idx and tidx
blocks.explain: When the next row continues the same AIR, air_idx is held constant, pv_idx increments by 1, and tidx increments by 1 — matching the linear layout of the child AIR's public values in the transcript. These together ensure all num_pvs values land at consecutive transcript indices starting from num_pvs_tidx.

blocks.heading: PublicValuesBus send
blocks.explain: Every valid row sends (air_idx, pv_idx, value) on PublicValuesBus. The downstream consumer is SymbolicExpressionAir, which looks up each public value at the correct (air_idx, pv_idx) during constraint evaluation. When continuations are enabled the message is sent twice, with the extra copy consumed by the enclosing continuations circuit that exposes public values.

blocks.heading: TranscriptBus observation of value
blocks.explain: The value cell drives both the PublicValuesBus send above and this TranscriptBus receive on the same row, at transcript index tidx with is_sample = 0 (observation). Because it's a single trace cell feeding both buses, the transcript-observed public values and the values used in constraint evaluation are guaranteed to be identical — exactly the invariant the reference verifier relies on when it observes the PVs before passing the same vector to verify_zerocheck_and_logup.
